run_summary: Screen 1 faces a portfolio stress test: 7 open positions underwater or barely up, with 5 at/past the 15-day holding window. Four fresh flags from the GPT-5.6 catalyst all meet BUY eligibility but trigger the basket rule — four OVERDONE names from one trigger defaults all to exploratory tier, with tight cash and sector headroom forcing WATCH on the weakest conviction signal.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
entries.thesis: GPT-5.6 is a model release, not a cloud infrastructure product. DOCN's business — cheap, developer-friendly compute for SMBs and indie developers underserved by hyperscalers — is orthogonal to LLM capabilities. If anything, more LLM apps means more demand for cheap inference and app-hosting compute, which DOCN provides. The sharp sympathy drop is thematic 'AI kills cloud SaaS' selling, not a filings-supported thesis.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of accelerating customer churn to AWS/GCP in DOCN's core SMB base, or a hyperscaler launching a genuine developer-simple pricing tier that eats into DOCN's positioning.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. GPT-5.6 is a model release; DOCN's developer-friendly IaaS (GPU droplets, cheap inference) is orthogonal and potentially benefits from more LLM apps. Filings explicitly frame AI/ML as growth vector for DOCN. Five-day move is +0.28%, suggesting panic is contained and not yet crowded. This is one of four OVERDONE flags from the GPT-5.6 catalyst, triggering the basket rule — all four default to exploratory tier (6% each) to avoid disguised 60% basket conviction. DOCN's filings evidence is strong ('A growing part of our business involves AI/ML') but no materially stronger than the peers, so remains exploratory. Exit prior DOCN positions first (they are past window); this is a fresh entry.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes DOCN's inference/hosting moat is defensible against hyperscalers' AI-driven pricing innovation, but recent hyperscaler moves into edge/small-instance compute are not explicitly addressed.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly identifies that model releases should drive inference demand, but underweights the risk that AWS/GCP/Azure will use GPT-5.6 as a wedge to bundle cheap AI inference into their free or low-cost tiers to lock in SMB developers. DOCN's filings cite AI/ML as a growth vector, but do not quantify the magnitude of inference workloads or defend against bundle risk. The five-day +0.28% move and 0.56x volume suggest the selloff is indeed shallow, which supports the fade thesis, but the absence of recent competitive response data from hyperscalers leaves timing risk unaddressed.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
entries.thesis: DUOL's moat is the dopamine loop (streaks, leagues, push-notification gamification, social graph), not instructional content quality. General-purpose LLM chat tutors have existed since GPT-4 and DUOL has continued growing MAU/DAU; GPT-5.6 is a marginal capability step, not a step-function replacement for the habit product. DUOL is also embedding GenAI (Max) as a monetization lever. The sharp reaction is reflex edtech-vs-ChatGPT trading.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of accelerating DAU decline, meaningful reduction in Max attach, or an OpenAI-native consumer language-learning app gaining traction with retention metrics comparable to DUOL.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings note GenAI as competitive risk but DUOL is itself a GenAI adopter (Duolingo Max); the core moat is the dopamine loop (streaks, leagues, social graph), not instructional quality. Five-day move is -0.8%, indicating the panic is shallow and not crowded in. GPT-5.6 is a marginal capability step, not a step-function substitute for the habit product. This is the second of four flags from the GPT-5.6 basket; tier is exploratory per basket rule. Filings evidence is moderate (explicit risk mention but offset by Max adoption), confirming exploratory is correct (not conviction). Buy 6% of equity.

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis rests on dopamine-loop defensibility, but does not quantify what fraction of DUOL's retention risk is driven by free/freemium AI tutors that have existed unmentioned in the filings.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly notes GPT-5.6 is incremental, not step-function, and cites DUOL's track record of surviving OpenAI releases. However, the five-day move of -0.8% and 0.42x volume indicate minimal panic is already baked in, and the thesis does not address whether OpenAI or a competitor has released a mobile-native language app with comparable retention metrics since DUOL's last 10-Q. If Max attach remains strong in the next print, this critique weakens; if Max attach stalled, the dopamine-loop moat claim needs re-examination.

entries.name: Tenable Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
entries.thesis: TENB's own filings argue that AI-accelerated exploit generation increases demand for continuous vulnerability scanning across hybrid infrastructure — the exact opposite of the panic narrative. Nessus agents collect data from customer environments; an LLM cannot substitute for the sensor footprint. TENB is also embedding GenAI (ExposureAI) as a prioritization layer. Sympathy drop is pure sector reflex.
entries.what_kills: A CrowdStrike/Palo Alto/Wiz bundle that meaningfully cannibalizes the standalone VM buyer, or a material customer disclosing they are replacing Nessus with an LLM-based scanner.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings explicitly argue AI increases demand for TENB's continuous vulnerability scanning (Nessus sensor footprint + FedRAMP/PCI moat). TENB has integrated GenAI itself (ExposureAI, Tenable AI Assistant), so the company is a beneficiary, not a victim. Five-day move is +3.06%, showing shallow panic and some recovery already underway. This is the third of four flags from the GPT-5.6 basket; tier is exploratory per basket rule. Filings are strong but not materially differentiated from QLYS or DUOL, confirming exploratory tier. Buy 6% of equity.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case cites TENB's filings as evidence that AI increases scanning demand, but does not reconcile the apparent contradiction that GPT-5.6 can generate exploits faster, which could compress the exploit-discovery window and devalue real-time scanning urgency.
red_team.critique: The bull case argues that faster exploit generation via LLMs increases scanning demand, but the inverse mechanism—that faster exploit discovery compresses the mean time to vulnerability and reduces the value of continuous scanning (since patches may already be in flight)—is not explicitly rebutted. TENB's five-day move of +3.06% already shows recovery, and filings evidence of AI as a tailwind is explicit in ExposureAI, but the filings do not address whether customers are extending scan intervals or deprioritizing agents in cloud-only infrastructure. The 0.67x volume is low, suggesting conviction is genuinely exploratory.

entries.name: Qualys, Inc.
entries.catalyst: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
entries.thesis: GPT-5.6 improving 'cybersecurity' does not replace continuous asset scanning, vulnerability enumeration, and compliance audit trail generation for regulated enterprises. Qualys's moat is the sensor footprint plus the FedRAMP/PCI/HIPAA-certified reporting pipeline — an LLM cannot substitute for the compliance artifact. The panic is thematic cybersecurity-sector selling on a headline mention of 'cybersecurity improvements.'
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler or OpenAI-partnered product actually launching an audit-grade vulnerability management offering with compliance certifications, or QLYS disclosing a real ARR headwind attributable to LLM-based competitors.
entries.reasoning: OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='indirect' (not 'minimal'), panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings support the thesis (compliance scanning + FedRAMP/PCI moat is not replaceable by LLM), but 'indirect' threat requires sharper filings evidence or a catalyst-specific volume signature to justify BUY. Five-day move is +3.09%, suggesting some recovery but not yet enough volume/conviction separation. Basket rule applies (four flags from GPT-5.6 trigger) but QLYS's 'indirect' threat assessment makes it the weakest in the quartet. WATCH pending either a deeper panic dip (move_pct < -6%) or fresh filings evidence that AI augments (not displaces) compliance scanning demand.
